title: oxytropis luteola
intro: Oxytropis luteola, also known as the yellow locoweed or locoweed, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and supporting immune function. Oxytropis luteola can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional Chinese medicine and other holistic practices.
